sections.heading: Block Access at the Organization Level
sections.body:
You must be an organization owner to adjust this setting.

Toggle the switch marked Block MongoDB Production
Support Employee Access to Atlas Infrastructure to the
On position.

sections.heading: Grant Infrastructure Access to MongoDB Support for 24 hours
sections.body:
If an issue that requires MongoDB Support arises and you want to allow
MongoDB support
staff limited-time access to a cluster
within your organization,
you can do so with the following procedure.

Atlas Administration API

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Grant Infrastructure Access to MongoDB Support for 24 hours
sections.body:
To grant limited-time access to MongoDB support staff to a cluster through
the Atlas Administration API, see [Grant MongoDB employee cluster access for one cluster](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-grantgroupclustermongodbemployeeaccess).

You can specify `CLUSTER_INFRASTRUCTURE` for the `grantType` field in the request body schema.

sections.heading: Grant Infrastructure Access to MongoDB Support for 24 hours
sections.body:
- Locate the cluster to which to grant access
  from the list of clusters.

- Select the ellipsis icon (...) to the right of the
  cluster.

- Select Grant Temporary Infrastructure Access to MongoDB Support.

- Select Atlas cluster infrastructure and all cluster logs.

- Click Grant Access.

sections.heading: Grant Access to MongoDB Support Only for Database Logs
sections.body:
If an issue that requires MongoDB Support arises and you want to allow
MongoDB support staff limited-time access only to database logs within
your organization, you can do so with the following procedure.

Atlas Administration API

Atlas UI

sections.heading: Grant Access to MongoDB Support Only for Database Logs
sections.body:
To grant access to MongoDB support staff to database logs through
the Atlas Administration API, see [Grant MongoDB employee cluster access for one cluster](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-grantgroupclustermongodbemployeeaccess).

You can specify `CLUSTER_DATABASE_LOGS` for the `grantType` field in the request body schema.

sections.heading: Grant Access to MongoDB Support Only for Database Logs
sections.body:
- Select General Settings from the Settings menu in the navigation bar.

- Toggle Block MongoDB Support Access to Atlas Cluster Infrastructure and Logs to `Yes`.

* Select the ellipsis icon (...) to the right of the cluster.

* Select Grant Temporary Infrastructure Access to MongoDB Support.

* Select Only database logs.

* Click Grant Access.

  IMPORTANT: You can grant MongoDB Support staff access only to database logs for 24 hours.
  Any downloads in progress won't be interrupted if you revoke this access. Database audit logs are
  not included in this access grant.

sections.heading: Revoke Temporary Infrastructure Access to MongoDB Support
sections.body:
If you want to revoke access to a cluster
within your organization that has been granted
24-hour temporary infrastructure access,
you can do so with the following procedure.

> **Important:** Temporary infrastructure access that is not revoked will automatically
> expire at the end of 24 hours. Atlas displays a timer indicating
> the amount of time left before 24-hour temporary infrastructure access expires.

> **Important:** Ensure that all issues requiring MongoDB Support have been
> addressed before revoking access.

Click Revoke Access to revoke temporary
infrastructure access.

To revoke 24-hour temporary infrastructure access from MongoDB support staff
through the Atlas Administration API, see [Revoke granted MongoDB employee
cluster access for one cluster](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-revokemongodbemployeeaccess).

sections.heading: Access Transparency Employee Identifiers
sections.body:
You can monitor certain actions on your infrastructure with an
anonymized **Employee Identifier**, which you can view in each
[Project Activity Feed](view-activity-feed) entry related to accessing
your infrastructure.

Each **Employee Identifier**:

- Starts with `mongodb-employee` and ends with a few digits.

- Is unique for each organization and MongoDB employee.

- Lasts for 30 days before Atlas automatically replaces it with a new identifier.

- Consists of a unique value that Atlas does not reuse for at least
  365 days from the day Atlas creates it.

> **Note:** If you have questions about employee access to your cluster
> infrastructure or logs, contact MongoDB Support.
